name: dumb-pypi
last_updated: 2026-05-19 (latest master commit; last tagged/PyPI release v1.15.0 on 2023-10-03 — recent commits are pre-commit.ci autoupdates only, no feature changes)
perf_notes: No server component: pip-facing latency/throughput is entirely provided by whatever static host serves the output (nginx, S3, CDN). The README states static file serving is "extremely fast" and that there are effectively no practical limits on the number of packages or versions. Index generation is a CLI script that the author describes as rebuilding "in seconds"; incremental rebuilds are supported via --previous-package-list / --previous-package-list-json. No published benchmarks.
scale_notes: Output is a tree of static HTML/JSON files. Because there is no app server, database, or cache, the index is inherently stateless and horizontally scalable: serve identical copies from any number of nginx nodes, from S3, or behind a CDN. The tool itself provides no HA/clustering, failover, or coordination logic — that is delegated to the static host. Recommended production pattern (per README): packages in S3, cron rebuild of the index, sync output to a serving bucket/nginx.
summary: A serverless, read-only private PyPI index generator that emits static PEP 503 simple HTML (plus a partial legacy JSON API) from a package list, to be served by any static webserver, S3, or CDN.
extras: Pure static-file generator with no server, database, or cache; installed as a Python package (pip install dumb-pypi) and run as a one-shot CLI

Two input formats: plain filename-per-line list, or JSON-lines with per-file metadata (hash <algo>=<value>, requires_python, core_metadata for PEP 658/714, yanked_reason for PEP 592, upload_timestamp, uploaded_by, requires_dist)
Incremental/partial rebuilds via --previous-package-list and --previous-package-list-json (only files for changed packages are rebuilt)

Simple HTML index emits data-requires-python, data-core-metadata (the PEP 714 attribute name), and data-yanked attributes (verified in templates/package.html); the .metadata sidecar and hashes must be supplied via input — dumb-pypi cannot inspect package files, so PEP 658/714 support is partial

Generates a legacy /pypi/<pkg>/json and per-version /pypi/<pkg>/<version>/json (opt-out via --no-per-release-json); the per-version endpoint returns only the requested version and omits many keys it cannot compute without inspecting packages. This is NOT the PEP 691 JSON Simple API and carries no PEP 700 versions/size/upload-time simple-index fields

All standard install clients resolve from the static PEP 503 index: pip, uv, Poetry, PDM, Hatch, and Pipenv install via the Simple API by pointing at /simple. uv installs fine but its --exclude-newer flag is non-functional because there is no PEP 700 upload-time data in the simple index, so uv is marked partial. twine and flit publish through the legacy upload API, which dumb-pypi does not provide, so neither can push to it
Sha256 (and other algos) embedded as URL hash fragments so pip/uv verify integrity

Ships a human-browsable web UI / landing page (index.html, package.html, changelog.html; live demo at chriskuehl.github.io/dumb-pypi/test-repo). It lists files and shows uploaded_by/upload_timestamp but does not render package READMEs/long descriptions

Does NOT host package files itself: artifacts live wherever --packages-url points (e.g. S3); dumb-pypi only generates the index referencing them and writes the index to a local --output-dir, so S3 is the documented deployment target rather than a native write backend

No JSON-based Simple API (PEP 691), no PEP 700 extensions, no PEP 629 api-version marker, and no PEP 740 attestations — only static HTML simple index files (/simple/index.html and /simple/<pkg>/index.html)
Auth/TLS/HA are entirely the static host's responsibility (e.g. nginx basic auth, S3 policies) — none are features of the tool
Companion project ghpypi adapts the same idea to GitHub Pages
Latest PyPI/tagged release is v1.15.0 (2023-10-03); master is maintained (last push 2026-05-19) but recent commits are pre-commit.ci autoupdates with no functional changes
